LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS SEVERINO CAMACLANG JR., EILEEN CAMACLANG'S MOTION TO STAY IS DENIED. PLAINTIFFS FAIL TO SHOW GOOD CAUSE FOR THEIR REQUESTED STAY. PLAINTIFFS HAVE NOT IDENTIFIED ANY ADMINISTRATIVE AGENCY CASES OR ANY OTHER CASES THAT ARE PENDING THAT MIGHT AFFECT THE RESOLUTION OF THIS CASE. PLAINTIFFS HAVE NOT PRESENTED ANY FACTS SHOWING THAT THERE ARE ANY SPECIAL CIRCUMSTANCES THAT SUGGEST THAT A STAY WOULD RESULT IN JUDICIAL ECONOMY OR REDUCED EXPENSE TO THE LITIGANTS. NOR HAVE PLAINTIFFS SHOWN THAT THE PRIMARY JURISDICTION DOCTRINE APPLIES TO THIS CASE. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR NO. 8189, EMAIL: CKAREN@SFTC.ORG

Off Calendar Apr-03-2017 Vacated

Motion To Stay And Notice Of Hearing
Off Calendar Mar-23-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
James W. Murray, of Thomas J. Murray & Associates, Tel. 419.616.0097, for plaintiffs;
Julie E. Hayashida, of Becherer Kannett & Schweitzer, Tel. 510.658.3600, for defendants Suzuki Motor Corporation.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF SEVERINO CAMACLANG Jr., EILEEN CAMACLANG's Motion To Stay is denied. Plaintiffs fail to show good cause for their requested stay. Plaintiffs have not identified any administrative agency cases or any other cases that are pending that might affect the resolution of this case. Plaintiffs have not presented any facts showing that there are any special circumstances that suggest that a stay would result in judicial economy or reduced expense to the litigants. Nor have plaintiffs shown that the primary jurisdiction doctrine applies to this case. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Hearing Date
Off Calendar Sep-08-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Severino Camaglang and Eileen Camaclang (707-696-8430) appear as plaintiffs/opposing parties In Propria Persona

Attorney James W. Murray, Esq. (415-640-0027) makes a special appearance as an attorney assisting the plaintiffs, not yet substituted in.

Attorney, Julie E. Hayashida of Becherer Kannett & Schweitzer (510-658-3600) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party, Suzuki Motors of America, Inc.

Counsel for the moving party updates the court on proceedings in the last three months. The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the court reporter. Court directs counsel to meet and confer off the record before recalling the matter. The Court rules in open forum as set forth in the transcript and as follows:

DEFENDANT SUZUKI MOTOR OF AMERICA, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication is GRANTED for reasons stated in the tentative ruling issued in advance of the June 16, 2016 hearing date. Prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order tracking verbatim the Court's ruling and in compliance with CCP 437c(g) and CRC 3.1312.

Also, an Ex Parte application to continue trial, scheduled in Department 206 for this date is deemed transferred and Granted. The Trial date of Scheduled for September 12 and October 3, 2016 is ordered continued to December 12, 2016 at 9:30 AM in Department 206. The applicant party must submit a [proposed] written order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR #59761, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-08-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Severino Camaglang and Eileen Camaclang (707-696-8430) appear as plaintiffs/opposing parties In Propria Persona

Attorney James W. Murray, Esq. (415-640-0027) makes a special appearance as an attorney assisting the plaintiffs, not yet substituted in.

Attorney, Julie E. Hayashida of Becherer Kannett & Schweitzer (510-658-3600) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party, Suzuki Motors of America, Inc.

Counsel for the moving part updates the court on proceedings in the last three months. The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the court reporter. Court directs counsel to meet and confer off the record before recalling the matter. The Court rules in open forum as set forth in the transcript and as follows:

DEFENDANT SUZUKI MOTOR OF AMERICA, INC. MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT is GRANTED except that if the plaintiffs have a good basis to bring a motion to amend to add a legally sufficient fraud claim against Suzuki Motors of America, Inc., they are not precluded from doing so. Prevailing party must submit a [proposed] written order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Also, an Ex Parte application to continue trial, scheduled in Department 206 for this date is deemed transferred and Granted. The Trial date of Scheduled for September 12 and October 3, 2016 is ordered continued to December 12, 2016 at 9:30 AM in Department 206. The applicant party must submit a [proposed] written order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR #59761, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-24-2016 Continued To Sep-07-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Severino Camaclang and Eileen Camaclang (707-696-8430) appear telephonically as plaintiffs/opposing parties, In Propria Persona

No other appearance is made in this matter.

DEFENDANT SUZUKI MOTOR OF AMERICA, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Continued on the court's own motion to September 7, 2016 at 9:30 AM in Department 302 to be heard concurrently with defendant Suzuki Motors of America, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment or, in the alternative, motion for summary adjudication. For guidance of the parties, the court believes that, even if plaintiffs should have sought leave to add a fraud claim against defendant Suzuki Motors of America, Inc., that defendant has not shown any prejudice by plaintiffs' failure to comply with that technical rule. In all events, the court believes that the issues raised by this motion are inextricably intertwined with the summary judgment/adjudication motion, that both motions should be heard at the same time.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR #6576, bus. tel: (530) 339-5023, email: rhunter@sftc.org. (D302)
